text: I stand in dimly lit, sterile hallways and stare into voids—glass walls reflecting empty office spaces. The harshness of artificial lights pierces the gloom. I feel disoriented, possibly watched, and I sense a disconnect from comfort or belonging. In the next room, large letters spelling 'R+D' loom over displays, but the scene is enveloped by darkness. This world feels impersonal, cold, and a bit foreboding—almost as if progress and development have pushed out warmth. My mind circles around the tension between innovation and alienation. I yearn for meaning amid the shadows.
